The report details changes in the City'shousingstock, includinghousingconstruction, demolition, and alterations, and has been published regularly since 1967.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "San Francisco, California Airbnb Market Data 2026: STR Report & Statistics", "url": "https://www.airroi.com/airbnb-data/united-states/california/san-francisco", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: San Francisco, California Airbnb Data 2026: Occupancy, Revenue & STR Market Report URL Source: https://www.airroi.com/airbnb-data/united-states/california/san-francisco Published Time: 2026-04-12 Markdown Content: 1. San Francisco Updated: 2026-04-12 How much can you earn on Airbnb in San Francisco, California? Based on AirROI's 2026 dataset (April 2025 \u2013 March 2026), the short answer is **$37,516 per year** \u2014 at a **$268 nightly rate**, **50.1% occupancy**, and a **$138 RevPAR** that reflects moderate rate-to-revenue efficiency with room to optimize. San Francisco's **4,089 active listings** and solid booking activity throughout the year make it a **major short-term rental market**. Supply grew **19.5%** over the past year, yet revenue and nightly rates both trended upward \u2014 a signal that traveler demand is outpacing new inventory rather than being diluted by it. For hosts, pricing power remains intact even as competition increases. **Regulation is high** and 69% of listings show active registration \u2014 compliance is the cost of entry. The data points to a market where fundamentals reward execution \u2014 the right property, priced well, in the right neighborhood still outperforms. ## What Are the Key Airbnb Metrics in San Francisco? In San Francisco, the headline Airbnb metrics are $37,516 in average annual revenue,50.1% occupancy, $268 ADR, and $138 in RevPAR, and guests book about 45 days in advance. Avg. Daily Rate (ADR)(?) The average rental revenue earned for an occupied room per day in San Francisco. Occupancy Rate(?) High demand. Good booking frequency. Avg. Annual Revenue(?) $37,516 Average annual income for an Airbnb listing in San Francisco. Factors like property type, size, and location influence actual earnings. RevPAR(?) Revenue per available rental night, combining occupancy and ADR into one efficiency metric. Revenue Growth YoY(?) Strong growth (23.2%) vs. last year.
